tomato.soil_suitability: Well-drained, sandy loam soil with pH 6.0 to 6.8 is optimal.
tomato.fertilizer_recommendation: Apply nitrogenous fertilizer (Urea) during the vegetative stage. Transition to phosphorus-rich fertilizers (DAP) during flowering and potassium-rich ones (Potash) during fruiting.
tomato.diseases: Early Blight (Alternaria solani), Late Blight (Phytophthora infestans), Leaf Mold, Tomato Leaf Curl Virus.
tomato.irrigation_habits: Requires moderate watering. Avoid overhead sprinklers; drip irrigation is strongly recommended to prevent foliar fungal spore splash.

cotton.sowing_period: Kharif season (April-May in North India, June-July in South/Central India)
cotton.soil_suitability: Deep black clay soils (regur soils) are optimal due to high water-retention properties. Medium loam is also suitable.
cotton.fertilizer_recommendation: Requires heavy nitrogen and phosphorus. Apply split doses of Nitrogen: 50% at sowing, 25% at square formation, 25% at boll development.
cotton.diseases: Boll rot, Bacterial blight, Fusarium wilt, Leaf curl disease.
cotton.irrigation_habits: Moderate watering; crucial stages are flowering and boll development. Highly sensitive to waterlogging.

wheat.soil_suitability: Well-drained clay loam or loamy soils are ideal.
wheat.fertilizer_recommendation: Standard ratio is N:P:K at 120:60:40 kg/ha. Apply half nitrogen + full P & K at sowing; apply remaining N during first irrigation.
wheat.diseases: Yellow rust, Brown rust, Black rust, Loose smut, Powdery mildew.
wheat.irrigation_habits: Requires 4-6 irrigations. Crown Root Initiation (CRI) stage (21 days post-sowing) is the most critical stage for watering.

rice.sowing_period: Kharif season (May-June for nursery, June-July for transplanting)
rice.soil_suitability: Clayey or clay loam soils that can retain standing water are optimal.
rice.fertilizer_recommendation: 100-120 kg Nitrogen, 50-60 kg P2O5, and 40 kg K2O per hectare. Zinc sulfate (25 kg/ha) should be applied to prevent Khaira disease.
rice.diseases: Blast (Magnaporthe oryzae), Bacterial leaf blight, Sheath blight, Khaira disease (zinc deficiency).
rice.irrigation_habits: Requires standing water (2-5 cm) from transplanting until 15 days before harvest. Submergence tolerant varieties should be used in flood-prone zones.

potato.ideal_temp_c: 15-20°C for tuberization. Tuber growth stops if temp exceeds 30°C.
potato.soil_suitability: Loose, well-aerated sandy loam rich in organic matter (pH 5.2-6.4).
potato.fertilizer_recommendation: Apply well-decomposed farmyard manure (25 t/ha). NPK requirement: 120-150 kg N, 80-100 kg P, 80-100 kg K per hectare.
potato.diseases: Early Blight, Late Blight, Black scurf, Common scab.
potato.irrigation_habits: Requires frequent shallow irrigations. Maintain uniform soil moisture to prevent tuber cracking and deformities.

blight.environmental_triggers: Cool, damp weather with high humidity (>90%) and frequent leaf wetness.
blight.chemical_control: Spray Mancozeb (2g/L) or Copper Oxychloride (3g/L) as a protective measure. Use Metalaxyl or Azoxystrobin for active infections.
blight.organic_control: Apply Trichoderma viride formulations or spray diluted neem oil (0.5%). Remove and burn infected foliage immediately.

rust.environmental_triggers: Cool temperatures (15-22°C) combined with dew or relative humidity.
rust.chemical_control: Spray Propiconazole (Tilt 25 EC) at 1ml/L or Tebuconazole when pustules first appear.
rust.organic_control: Grow rust-resistant cultivars. Apply sulfur dust or copper sulfate sprays early in the morning.

fertilizer.urea: 46% Nitrogen. Promotes rapid vegetative growth. High leaf chlorophyll density.
fertilizer.dap: 18% Nitrogen, 46% Phosphorus. Supports early root establishment and robust flowering.
fertilizer.potash: 60% Potassium (MOP). Enhances plant immune systems, crop weight, sugar accumulation, and drought tolerance.
fertilizer.npk_general: Apply balanced ratios according to regional crop recommendations. Excess nitrogen causes watery stems and invites pest attacks.

soil_ph.acidic_fix: Apply agricultural lime (calcium carbonate) or dolomite lime. Dolomite is preferred if soil test indicates magnesium deficiency.
soil_ph.alkaline_fix: Incorporate elemental sulfur or gypsum. Organic mulch and compost buffer alkaline soils by generating weak acids during decomposition.
soil_ph.nutrient_availability: Optimal nutrient assimilation occurs between pH 6.0 and 7.0. Iron and zinc become locked in highly alkaline soils.
